content.text:
Module:
Advanced Algorithmics and Complexity  |
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEF11
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
1h30  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Understand the notions of algorithmic complexity (time, space)
Master advanced algorithm design techniques
Classify problems according to their difficulty (P, NP, NP-complete, etc.)
Apply exact and approximate methods to complex problems
Recommended Prerequisites
Basics in algorithmics and data structures
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Algorithmic Complexity
Asymptotic notations (Big O, Ω, Θ)
Best-case, worst-case, and average-case analysis
Recurrence equations
Advanced Data Structures and Complexity
Binary trees (review), AVL trees, Red-Black trees
Hash tables
Textual Algorithmics and Complexity
Pattern searching
Compression algorithms
Design Paradigms
Divide and conquer
Dynamic programming
Greedy algorithms
Probabilistic algorithms
Problem Classes
Decision and optimization problems
Determinism vs non-determinism
NP-completeness and problem reduction
Advanced Algorithms
Parallel and distributed algorithms
Online algorithms
Randomized algorithms
Assessment
Exam: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
T. Cormen, C. Leiserson, R. Rivest & C. Stein (2022). Introduction to Algorithms. The MIT Press, 5th Edition.
P. Brass (2008). Advanced Data Structures. Cambridge University Press, City College of New York.
C. A. Shaffer (2010). A Practical Introduction to Data Structures and Algorithm Analysis. Department of Computer Science, Virginia Tech.
Sedgewick, R., & Wayne, K. (2011). Algorithms (4th ed.). Addison-Wesley.
Kozen, D. C. (1991). The Design and Analysis of Algorithms. Springer.
Motwani, R., & Raghavan, P. (1995). Randomized Algorithms. Cambridge University Press.
S. Arora & B. Barak (2006). Computational Complexity: A Modern Approach. Cambridge University Press.
